text:
AI Developer Radar｜Daily｜2026-06-08

今日结论：
AI agent frameworks are the dominant category today, with 7 repos in the top 100 and an average daily delta of 1,790 stars, led by zhayujie/CowAgent (45,124 stars, +45 daily) and automazeio/ccpm (8,175 stars, +6 daily). A notable acceleration anomaly is zilliztech/claude-context, which jumped 4.7x in daily star velocity (58 stars in 24h vs 134 in 7d), indicating sudden developer interest in combining vector databases with MCP for semantic code search. The category 'Coding Agent / SWE Agent' also shows strong weekly momentum (avg 4,754 stars/week) but only 2 repos, suggesting concentrated interest. Meanwhile, distributed training infrastructure (hpcaitech/ColossalAI) saw a 6x acceleration but with very low absolute numbers (4 daily stars), likely a noise spike. The absence of any cross-source signals (no Product Hunt launches or HN front-page AI projects) suggests today's attention is purely GitHub-driven, possibly reflecting organic developer discovery rather than marketing pushes.

今日精选项目
1. zilliztech/claude-context
   GitHub: https://github.com/zilliztech/claude-context
   Category: Agentic RAG / Code Generation
   Stars: 11,779 (24h +58, 7d +134)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 4.7x (high, 前 3 日均值 12.3)
   Score: 71 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: An MCP plugin that gives AI coding agents semantic code search over your entire codebase using a vector database.
   Why worth watching: With 11,661 stars, 860 forks, and a sustained 7-day average of 15.1 stars/day (acceleration 1.3), this project is in a steady hot trend. The 24h delta of 16 stars and 7d delta of 105 indicate consistent, organic growth without sudden spikes, suggesting broad adoption among developers using AI coding tools.
   Developer takeaway: This signals a shift toward agentic RAG workflows in development: instead of feeding entire codebases to LLMs, developers will increasingly rely on semantic indexing to retrieve relevant context on demand. The MCP ecosystem is maturing, and vector databases are becoming a standard infrastructure layer for AI coding.
   Problem solved: AI coding assistants like Claude Code lack context of large codebases, requiring expensive multi-round discovery or loading entire directories. Claude Context solves this by indexing the codebase into a vector database and retrieving only relevant code snippets via semantic search, reducing cost and improving accuracy.
   What changed: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent commits (pushed May 22, 2026) and README updates (adding support for multiple MCP clients like Cursor, Gemini CLI, Qwen Code) likely contributed to continued interest.
   Target users: Developers using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, etc.) who work with large codebases and want to improve context quality while controlling API costs.
   Risk notes: Dependency on external services (Zilliz Cloud, OpenAI API) introduces vendor lock-in and potential costs at scale. The project is backed by Zilliz, which may bias toward their cloud offering. No recent release tag suggests the project may be in active development with potential instability.
   LLM confidence: high

2. hpcaitech/ColossalAI
   GitHub: https://github.com/hpcaitech/ColossalAI
   Category: Distributed Training & Inference Infrastructure
   Stars: 41,389 (24h +4, 7d +6)
   Trend: 突然爆火 | Acceleration: 6x (high, 前 3 日均值 0.7)
   Score: 42 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: Colossal-AI is an open-source deep learning system that makes large AI models cheaper, faster, and more accessible through advanced parallelism and heterogeneous memory management.
   Why worth watching: The repo has a sudden breakout with an acceleration of 6 (high), despite a low 7-day average star delta of -0.6. With 41,385 total stars and 4,505 forks, it's a well-established project that recently saw a spike in attention, possibly due to new benchmarks (B200 GPUs showing 50-70% throughput gains) or the release of Open-Sora and DeepSeek fine-tuning guides.
   Developer takeaway: This signals a shift toward democratizing large model training: developers can now achieve near-linear scaling on multi-GPU setups with minimal code changes, reducing the barrier to entry for building foundation models.
   Problem solved: Training and deploying large AI models (e.g., LLMs, video generation models) is prohibitively expensive and complex. Colossal-AI reduces cost and time by providing efficient distributed training and inference with minimal code changes.
   What changed: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. However, recent README updates highlight new benchmarks on B200 GPUs, DeepSeek 671B fine-tuning, and Open-Sora video generation, which may have driven the breakout.
   Target users: AI researchers, ML engineers, and startups working on large-scale model training or inference, especially those using PyTorch and seeking cost-effective GPU utilization.
   Risk notes: The project is backed by a company (HPC-AI Tech) that also offers paid cloud services, which may lead to feature prioritization for commercial users. Additionally, the rapid pace of change in AI infrastructure could make some parallelism strategies obsolete.
   LLM confidence: high

3. automazeio/ccpm
   GitHub: https://github.com/automazeio/ccpm
   Category: AI Agent Framework
   Stars: 8,175 (24h +6, 7d +16)
   Trend: 突然爆火 | Acceleration: 3.6x (high, 前 3 日均值 1.7)
   Score: 37 | Risk: Medium
   One-liner: CCPM is an Agent Skill that turns AI coding agents into structured project managers using GitHub Issues and parallel Git worktrees for spec-driven, multi-agent development.
   Why worth watching: With 8,166 stars, a 7-day average delta of 2.4, and a sudden acceleration to 2.4 (high), CCPM is experiencing a breakout. The 24-hour delta of 4 and 7-day delta of 17 indicate sustained, accelerating interest. This is not a flash spike but a rapidly growing project with strong community signals.
   Developer takeaway: This signals a shift from ad-hoc AI coding to disciplined, spec-driven development. The emphasis on 'no vibe coding' and full traceability suggests that the next frontier for AI coding tools is not just code generation but project management and accountability. Developers should watch for similar patterns in other tools.
   Problem solved: AI coding sessions lose context, block on sequential tasks, and produce untraceable code. CCPM enforces a PRD→Epic→Issue→Code pipeline with parallel agent execution and full traceability via GitHub Issues.
   What changed: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The high acceleration and sudden_breakout classification suggest recent external mentions (e.g., social media, newsletters) or word-of-mouth from early adopters. The README mentions integration with multiple AI harnesses, which may have expanded its reach.
   Target users: AI-assisted developers and teams using agent-based coding tools (Claude Code, Codex, Factory, etc.) who want structured project management, parallel execution, and traceability. Also relevant for open-source maintainers and solo developers managing complex projects.
   Risk notes: The project is early-stage (no recent release, no recent commits within 7 days). The high star count may be driven by hype rather than proven reliability. Dependency on GitHub Issues and `gh` CLI may limit adoption for teams using other platforms. The 'no vibe coding' philosophy may not suit all workflows.
   LLM confidence: high

4. zhayujie/CowAgent
   GitHub: https://github.com/zhayujie/CowAgent
   Category: AI Agent Framework
   Stars: 45,124 (24h +45, 7d +158)
   Trend: 突然爆火 | Acceleration: 2.9x (high, 前 3 日均值 15.3)
   Score: 72 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: CowAgent is an open-source AI assistant framework that plans tasks, runs tools and skills, and grows autonomously with memory and knowledge, supporting multiple LLMs and messaging channels.
   Why worth watching: With 45,118 stars, a 24h delta of +39, and a high acceleration ratio of 2.5, the project is experiencing a sudden breakout in attention. The 7-day average delta of 18.4 and recent release (v2.1.0 on 2026-06-01) indicate strong momentum.
   Developer takeaway: This signals a shift toward all-in-one agent frameworks that prioritize ease of deployment and extensibility. Developers should consider CowAgent for rapid prototyping of agentic workflows, especially those requiring memory, tool use, and multi-platform messaging.
   Problem solved: It provides a lightweight, extensible Agent Harness that integrates planning, memory, knowledge, tools, and multi-channel communication, enabling developers to deploy a personal AI assistant that can control computers and external services.
   What changed: The recent v2.1.0 release added internationalization, new channels (Telegram, Discord, Slack, WeChat Customer Service), CLI upgrades, streamlined one-line install, and MCP Streamable HTTP support, likely driving the spike.
   Target users: AI developers, hobbyists, and teams looking to deploy a personal or enterprise AI assistant with planning, memory, and tool-use capabilities across multiple messaging platforms.
   Risk notes: The project has a large codebase and may have a learning curve for customizing advanced features. As a relatively new framework, long-term maintenance and community support are still evolving. The high star count may attract attention but also potential security scrutiny.
   LLM confidence: high

5. thedaviddias/Front-End-Checklist
   GitHub: https://github.com/thedaviddias/Front-End-Checklist
   Category: AI Agent Framework
   Stars: 72,859 (24h +29, 7d +109)
   Trend: 突然爆火 | Acceleration: 2.1x (high, 前 3 日均值 13.7)
   Score: 61 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: A comprehensive, MCP-powered front-end quality checklist with 385 rules across 11 categories, designed for both human review and AI agent workflows.
   Why worth watching: With 72,789 stars, a 24-hour star delta of 18, and a high acceleration of 2.5, the project is experiencing a sudden breakout in attention. The recent release and active commits (within 7 days) suggest ongoing development, while the MCP server integration positions it as a key tool for AI-assisted code review.
   Developer takeaway: This signals a shift toward structured, rule-based AI agents that can enforce best practices autonomously. Developers should consider building similar MCP servers for their own standards to enable agent-driven quality assurance.
   Problem solved: Provides a structured, auditable front-end best practices system that developers and AI agents can use to ensure code quality, accessibility, performance, and security, reducing the need to memorize or manually enforce standards.
   What changed: The project recently added an MCP server (mcp.frontendchecklist.io) and skills system, enabling AI agents to use the checklist directly. The README now emphasizes agent workflows, and a companion project (UX Patterns for Devs) was launched. These updates likely drove the sudden spike in stars.
   Target users: Front-end developers, web developers, QA engineers, and AI agent developers who want to automate front-end quality checks.
   Risk notes: The checklist is opinionated and may not cover all edge cases. Over-reliance on automated checks could miss context-specific issues. The MCP server is hosted externally, so availability and latency depend on the provider.
   LLM confidence: high

6. rohitg00/ai-engineering-from-scratch
   GitHub: https://github.com/rohitg00/ai-engineering-from-scratch
   Category: AI Agent Framework
   Stars: 29,853 (24h +977, 7d +8753)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 2.7x (high, 前 3 日均值 356)
   Score: 87 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: A massive, free, open-source curriculum (503 lessons, 20 phases) that teaches AI engineering from math foundations to autonomous swarms, with every lesson producing a reusable artifact.
   Why worth watching: With 29,645 stars, a 24-hour delta of +769, and a 7-day average of +2,592 stars/day, this repo is experiencing sustained, high-velocity growth. The acceleration of 0.3 indicates steady momentum rather than a sudden spike, suggesting deep, organic interest from the developer community. The 150k+ readers and 241k page views in the last 30 days confirm broad engagement.
   Developer takeaway: The trend signals a shift in developer demand: from surface-level API usage to deep, foundational understanding. Projects that offer structured, hands-on, and artifact-driven learning are gaining traction. This repo also highlights the value of combining multiple languages (Python, TypeScript, Rust, Julia) in a single curriculum.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: Most AI learning resources are scattered, shallow, or API-focused, leaving learners unable to understand or build AI systems from the ground up. This curriculum provides a structured, end-to-end path that bridges theory and practice, ensuring learners can explain and implement what they use.
   What changed: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The consistent daily star accumulation and high reader numbers point to ongoing word-of-mouth and social sharing, likely amplified by the curriculum's comprehensive scope and practical artifacts.
   Target users: Developers and engineers who want to master AI engineering from first principles, including those already using AI tools but feeling unprepared for professional work. Also relevant for students, self-learners, and teams building internal AI capabilities.
   Risk notes: The curriculum is massive (~320 hours), which may overwhelm some users. Completion rates for such large open-source courses are typically low. The project is maintained by a single creator (rohitg00), so long-term sustainability depends on continued contributions and community support.
   LLM confidence: high

7. esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix
   GitHub: https://github.com/esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix
   Category: Coding Agent / SWE Agent
   Stars: 19,206 (24h +653, 7d +4583)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 1x (high, 前 3 日均值 629.3)
   Score: 82 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: A DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for the terminal, rewritten in Go for performance and prefix-cache stability.
   Why worth watching: With 19,206 stars, a 24-hour delta of +653, and a 7-day average of +652 stars per day, this project is experiencing sustained high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio is 1 (steady, not spiking), indicating organic, community-driven momentum rather than a viral event. It ranks top 2 in Agents by velocity on oosmetrics.
   Developer takeaway: The project signals a shift toward model-specific agents that optimize for API caching and cost efficiency. Developers building AI tools should consider prefix-cache-aware designs and single-binary distribution for adoption.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: Reduces token costs and latency for AI-assisted coding by leveraging DeepSeek's prefix caching, while providing a configurable, plugin-driven agent that runs as a single static binary.
   What changed: The project recently released Reasonix 1.0, a ground-up rewrite in Go (from TypeScript), which likely improved performance and reduced friction. The README highlights this migration and the new Go binary distribution, which may have attracted users seeking a more robust tool.
   Target users: Developers who want a fast, cost-effective AI coding agent in the terminal, especially those using DeepSeek models or seeking an open-source alternative to Cursor/GitHub Copilot.
   Risk notes: Heavy reliance on DeepSeek's API and prefix-cache behavior; changes to DeepSeek's API could impact performance. The project is still early (1.0), and 223 open issues suggest active development but also potential instability.
   LLM confidence: high

8. santifer/career-ops
   GitHub: https://github.com/santifer/career-ops
   Category: AI Workflow Automation
   Stars: 49,803 (24h +889, 7d +1855)
   Trend: 突然爆火 | Acceleration: 5.1x (high, 前 3 日均值 172.7)
   Score: 80 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: An AI-powered job search command center that uses Claude Code and other AI coding CLIs to evaluate, tailor, and track job applications at scale.
   Why worth watching: With 49,776 stars and a 24-hour delta of 862 (acceleration 5, well above the 2.0 threshold), this repo is experiencing a sudden breakout. The 7-day average delta of 152.4 and a 3-day average of 172.7 confirm sustained high velocity. The project has been featured in WIRED and Business Insider, and the author claims it helped land a Head of Applied AI role after evaluating 740+ listings. The recent release and active commits (within 7 days) keep momentum high.
   Developer takeaway: This signals a shift toward AI-assisted job search becoming a mainstream developer tool. The project's architecture—using AI CLIs as orchestrators rather than building custom models—shows a pragmatic path for AI workflow automation. Developers should watch for similar 'AI command center' patterns in other domains.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: Automates the tedious parts of job hunting—evaluating hundreds of listings, generating ATS-optimized CVs, and tracking pipeline—so candidates can focus on the few roles worth their time.
   What changed: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. However, the recent press coverage (WIRED, Business Insider) and the author's case study likely amplified visibility. The addition of Gemini CLI integration and multi-language README also broadened appeal.
   Target users: Job seekers in tech, especially those targeting AI/ML, engineering, and product roles; developers interested in AI agent orchestration; open-source contributors looking for a high-impact project.
   Risk notes: The system relies on third-party AI CLIs (Claude Code, Gemini CLI) which may change pricing or availability. The 'spray-and-pray' warning indicates potential misuse if users ignore the scoring recommendations. The project's rapid growth may strain maintenance and community support.
   LLM confidence: high

9. nexu-io/open-design
   GitHub: https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design
   Category: Coding Agent / SWE Agent
   Stars: 60,871 (24h +1358, 7d +4924)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 2.1x (high, 前 3 日均值 640)
   Score: 79 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: Open Design is a local-first, open-source alternative to Claude Design that turns your coding agent into a design engine, generating prototypes, decks, images, and videos from a single design system.
   Why worth watching: With 60,781 stars and a 7-day average delta of 567.6 stars/day, Open Design is sustaining explosive growth. The 24-hour delta of 1,268 stars and acceleration ratio of 2.0 indicate a sudden surge in attention, likely driven by the 0.9.0 release that bundles an official Model Router for zero-setup use. This project is riding the wave of agent-native design tools and the broader shift toward local-first, open-source alternatives to proprietary AI products.
   Developer takeaway: This signals a workflow shift: design is becoming a programmable, agent-driven activity. Developers building AI tools should prioritize local-first architectures and agent interoperability (e.g., MCP) to capture this growing market. The project's rapid adoption also highlights the risk of ignoring open-source alternatives to popular proprietary tools.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: It replaces proprietary, cloud-dependent design tools with an agent-native, filesystem-based design workflow where skills, design systems, and plugins are local files that any coding agent can read, write, and remix — enabling brand-consistent, exportable artifacts without leaving the CLI or paying per-seat.
   What changed: The release of Open Design 0.9.0 with an integrated Model Router (no API key or CLI setup required) is the most likely trigger. The README prominently features this as a major milestone, and the high acceleration aligns with a new release that lowers the barrier to entry.
   Target users: Developers, designers, and product builders who want to generate brand-consistent prototypes, decks, and assets using their existing coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) without leaving the terminal or paying for per-seat design tools.
   Risk notes: The project is still in early stages (0.9.0) with 344 open issues, which may indicate stability or completeness gaps. As an open-source alternative to a popular proprietary tool, it faces ongoing competition and potential IP/legal challenges. The rapid growth may also strain community support and documentation.
   LLM confidence: high

10. mvanhorn/last30days-skill
   GitHub: https://github.com/mvanhorn/last30days-skill
   Category: AI Agent Framework
   Stars: 30,717 (24h +2440, 7d 暂无基线)
   Trend: 突然爆火 | Acceleration: 4.8x (medium, 前 3 日均值 504)
   Score: 78 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: An AI agent skill that searches Reddit, X, YouTube, HN, Polymarket, and the web in parallel, scoring results by real engagement (upvotes, likes, money) to produce a grounded, synthesized brief on any topic.
   Why worth watching: The repo exploded with 1,511 stars in the last 24 hours (538 yesterday, 504 three-day average) and an acceleration ratio of 3, indicating a sudden breakout. With 29,788 total stars and 2,512 forks, it's clearly hitting a nerve. The v3 release (intelligent search, shareable HTML briefs, cross-source clustering) likely triggered the surge, combined with strong community adoption via Claude Code and other agent hosts.
   Developer takeaway: The success of /last30days signals a shift toward agent-driven, multi-platform research tools that bypass traditional search. For AI developers, the opportunity is in building similar 'bridge' agents that connect walled gardens (social APIs, prediction markets, niche forums) and rank by real-world signals. The v3 intelligent pre-research pattern—resolving entities before searching—is a key architectural insight.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: Traditional search engines and individual AI assistants are siloed—they can't simultaneously access Reddit comments, X posts, YouTube transcripts, TikTok engagement, and Polymarket odds. This skill bridges those walled gardens, letting an AI agent search all of them at once and rank results by actual human attention rather than SEO.
   What changed: The v3 pipeline was recently released, introducing intelligent pre-research (resolves topics to specific people, subreddits, handles before searching), cross-source cluster merging, single-pass comparisons, auto-discovered competitor comparisons, and shareable HTML briefs. These features dramatically improve the quality and speed of results, making the skill more useful and viral.
   Target users: Developers, researchers, product managers, sales professionals, journalists, and anyone who needs to quickly gauge public sentiment or track a topic across social media, news, and prediction markets.
   Risk notes: The project relies on third-party APIs and browser sessions for some sources (X, YouTube, TikTok), which could break or require maintenance. The 105 open issues suggest active development but also potential instability. The sudden breakout may attract copycats or lead to API rate limiting if usage spikes too fast.
   LLM confidence: high

8. Her · हेर - Claude Code 会话分析工具
   Source: aihot | Type: curated_trend
   URL: https://huggingface.co/blog/build-small-hackathon/her-blog
   Signal captured
   Category: products
   Summary: Her（हेर）是专为 Claude Code 设计的会话分析工具。用户上传 `.jsonl` 文件后，Her 用自然语言重建每轮交互，标记部署、配置变更、秘密等高风险操作并定位到具体轮次。它展示 token 消耗、所用工具、子智能体、技能和 MCP 服务器，并结合 Anthropic 与社区最佳实践给出改进建议（仅在有明确可修复模式时）。内置"Ask Her"问答功能，支持单会话与跨会话项目分析。工具不调用第三方 AI API，使用 Nemotron-Mini-4B-Instruct 模型在 Hugging Face ZeroGPU 上运行，评估引擎完全确定，模型仅负责文本生成与建议。Her 内置 Homebrew、npm、PyPI 主流 CLI 工具数据库，自动识别会话中使用的工具，并对部署工具、数据库客户端等执行活动进行标记提醒。
   Original: Hugging Face：Blog（RSS） - https://huggingface.co/blog/build-small-hackathon/her-blog
   Why it matters: Her（हेर）是专为 Claude Code 设计的会话分析工具。用户上传 `.jsonl` 文件后，Her 用自然语言重建每轮交互，标记部署、配置变更、秘密等高风险操作并定位到具体轮次。它展示 token 消耗、所用工具、子智能体、技能和 MCP 服务器，并结合 Anthropic 与社区最佳实践给出改进建议（仅在有明确可修复模式时）。内置"Ask Her"问答功能，支持单会话与跨会话项目分析。工具不调用第三方 AI API，使用 Nemotron-Mini-4B-Instruct 模型在 Hugging Face ZeroGPU 上运行，评估引擎完全确定，模型仅负责文本生成与建议。Her 内置 Homebrew、npm、PyPI 主流 CLI 工具数据库，自动识别会话中使用的工具，并对部署工具、数据库客户端等执行活动进行标记提醒。

9. NVIDIA 与 KRAFTON、NC、T1 在韩国 PC 房庆祝 RTX Spark 发布
   Source: aihot | Type: curated_trend
   URL: https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/krafton-nc-t1-korea-gaming-pc-bang-rtx-spark
   Signal captured
   Category: products
   Summary: 继上周 GTC Taipei 发布后，NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang 前往韩国，向当地游戏社区正式介绍 RTX Spark 超芯片。该芯片集成 30 年 NVIDIA 技术，可在 Windows 笔记本上实现全天续航，并以 1440p 分辨率、超过 100fps 运行 AAA 游戏，支持包括 DLSS 4.5 Ray Reconstruction（基于第二代 Transformer 模型）在内的全套 RTX 技术。KRAFTON、NC 与 Riot Games 正合作将旗下游戏适配 RTX Spark。周五，Huang 在 T1 电竞场馆与 Faker 共同亮相，并突袭江南区网吧，演示《PUBG： BATTLEGROUNDS》及基于 NVIDIA ACE 的"PUBG Ally"AI 队友、《CINDER CITY》《AION 2》等作品。《CINDER CITY》首发支持 DLSS 4.5 动态多帧生成与超分辨率。已有超过 100 家 Windows 软件与游戏开发商采纳 RTX Spark。
   Original: NVIDIA AI Blog - https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/krafton-nc-t1-korea-gaming-pc-bang-rtx-spark
   Why it matters: 继上周 GTC Taipei 发布后，NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang 前往韩国，向当地游戏社区正式介绍 RTX Spark 超芯片。该芯片集成 30 年 NVIDIA 技术，可在 Windows 笔记本上实现全天续航，并以 1440p 分辨率、超过 100fps 运行 AAA 游戏，支持包括 DLSS 4.5 Ray Reconstruction（基于第二代 Transformer 模型）在内的全套 RTX 技术。KRAFTON、NC 与 Riot Games 正合作将旗下游戏适配 RTX Spark。周五，Huang 在 T1 电竞场馆与 Faker 共同亮相，并突袭江南区网吧，演示《PUBG： BATTLEGROUNDS》及基于 NVIDIA ACE 的"PUBG Ally"AI 队友、《CINDER CITY》《AION 2》等作品。《CINDER CITY》首发支持 DLSS 4.5 动态多帧生成与超分辨率。已有超过 100 家 Windows 软件与游戏开发商采纳 RTX Spark。
